project_overview.project_name: Umbrella Organization to Support NFWP (Nigeria For Women Project)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To support Nigeria for Women in implementing women's social and economic empowerment activities in targeted areas.
project_overview.beneficiaries: NFWP Federal and State Project Coordination Units (FPCU and SPCUs) for technical support; Women Affinity Group (WAG) members in select States and Local Government Areas for piloting health, GBV, livelihoods, and social norms interventions

primary_locations: Additional states under NFWP Scale Up (to be determined)
geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on six initial states and expansion to up to 20 states
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Northeast Nigeria (Taraba) experiences conflict-related insecurity; document notes heightened insecurity in Nigeria generally and conflict in northeast with Boko Haram activities affecting women and children; security risk management plan referenced

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Identification and Deployment of Technical Experts to Support NFWP
key_components.description: Deployment of approximately 8 full-time technical experts for 12-18 months to provide capacity building to NFWP FPCU and SPCUs. Expertise areas include behavioral change communications, institutional capacity building, gender and GBV, innovations and partnerships, livelihoods, financial inclusion, savings groups, and social norms marketing. Includes development of terms of reference, identification, evaluation, contracting, orientation, supervision, and performance evaluation of experts.

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Contracting and Management of Specialized Agencies
key_components.description: Implementation of three sub-components through Plan and State-level CSO partners: (2.1) Health and GBV training and communications leveraging WAGs as platforms; (2.2) Livelihoods pilot support including market scoping and analysis; (2.3) Social norms and communications innovations including testing of behavior change approaches

key_components.component_name: Sub-component 2.1: Leveraging WAGs as platforms for improved health and GBV prevention
key_components.description: Development and adaptation of health and GBV curricula, training of master facilitators and local facilitators, implementation of dialogue sessions with WAG members on health topics (antenatal care, immunization, family planning, hygiene) and GBV awareness raising including gender dialogue groups. Testing of delivery approaches and effectiveness through pre- and post-training assessments.

key_components.component_name: Sub-component 2.2: Leveraging WAGs as platforms for improved livelihoods
key_components.description: Market scoping and analysis of NFWP livelihoods interventions, consultations and focus group discussions with WAG members, identification of livelihoods pilot innovations, technical assistance to PCUs for implementation of identified innovations. Aims to identify entry points for improved livelihoods innovations before NFWP Scale Up implementation.

key_components.component_name: Sub-component 2.3: Social Norms at the community level
key_components.description: Review and improvement of existing social norms messaging used by NFWP States, development and implementation of social norms and communications innovations, testing of effectiveness through local facilitators. Pilot testing of time- and resource-intensive social norms change approaches (e.g., Tostan CEP model, IRC EA$E model, Save the Children CAC) in select LGAs. Coordination with NFWP Impact Evaluation sampling strategy.

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Project Management, Monitoring and Evaluation
key_components.description: Project management by Plan International Nigeria at Federal and State levels including supervision of CSO partners. Financing of M&E strategy and system linked to NFWP M&E and PMIS. Monthly review meetings on output indicators, quarterly reviews with World Bank and NFWP, data sharing with NFWP PCUs, documentation of lessons learned.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Plan International Nigeria, selected through competitive call for proposals modeled on World Bank's Global Partnership for Social Accountability (GPSA). Plan is an independent NGO operating in 75 countries with 80+ years experience. In Nigeria since 2014 with annual budget of US$32.8M, 250+ staff, presence in 18 states.
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Close collaboration with NFWP FPCU and SPCUs through: embedding Plan staff in SPCUs; co-creation of workplans and activity designs; regular coordination meetings with NFWP and other actors; monthly review meetings on output indicators; quarterly reviews with World Bank and NFWP; monthly and quarterly data sharing with NFWP PCUs; documentation and sharing of lessons learned with NFWP knowledge management team. Multi-stakeholder approach leveraging State Ministry of Women Affairs platforms and NFWP institutional arrangements.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Procurement in accordance with World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised November 2017, August 2018, November 2020). Plan's procurement policy based on fairness, transparency, accountability, ethical standards, equal opportunity, and value for money. Procurements above N8,000,000 advertised in minimum two national newspapers. Complaint-handling through email monitored by Country Director. Segregation of duties across four threshold levels. Market surveys conducted semi-annually. Outcomes communicated to service providers within 10 days. Contract Tracker maintained to prevent cost and time overruns. Procurement risk rated Moderate with mitigation measures including training on new Procurement Framework and STEP system, hands-on support for PPSD preparation.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: M&E strategy and system linked to NFWP M&E and PMIS. Plan's M&E system reports on Results Framework and collects data on Component 2 activities shared with NFWP M&E system. Monthly review meetings on output indicators involving local partners, NFWP, and World Bank. Quarterly reviews with World Bank and NFWP to assess progress against Results Framework. Monthly and quarterly data sharing with NFWP PCUs in agreed formats. Ongoing evaluation of Component 2 activities efficacy to inform NFWP Scale Up strategies. Responsive Feedback Mechanism (RFM) being established at NFWP to use project data for progress assessment and course correction. Dedicated M&E Coordinator deployed for grant management.

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks may arise from Plan's operating modality in Nigeria which entails working through locally-based CSOs at State level as sub-grantees or contracted partners, potentially affecting quality and consistency of implementation.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Plan project officers will be embedded in SPCUs at State level to ensure constant connection between Plan, CSO partners, and SPCU. FPCU and SPCUs will be key partners in selection of CSO partners, co-creation of workplans, and monitoring of grant activities.

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks to personal and project data may arise as Plan will access and collect WAG member data. Poor data access and management procedures related to linking with NFWP M&E system and MIS could compromise data security.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Robust data collection, retention, and protection policies will be developed with NFWP to ensure secure data sharing and storage across the two projects.

identified_risks.risk_description: Falling political will as election period approaches (February 2023 general elections mentioned), pressures to influence selection of local structures, potential cooption of WAGs for political purposes.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Work with NFWP to ensure WAGs are not coopted for political purposes and that NFWP structures for formation and training of WAGs remain operational throughout elections. Communications campaigns by NFWP to WAGs critical. Women's economic empowerment likely to remain priority for any incoming administration given its role in inclusive economic growth.

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential spread of COVID-19 during implementation of capacity building activities. Project-induced Sexual Exploitation and Abuse/Sexual Harassment (SEA/SH) issues perpetrated by contracted trainers and staff.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Implementation of measures acceptable to World Bank per Nigeria Centre for Disease Control, WHO, and World Bank guidelines on COVID-19 (social distancing, masks, hand washing, sanitizing). Labour Management Plan (LMP) prepared with Codes of Conduct for project contractors and workers addressing SEA/SH behavior and sanctions for infractions. Reinforcement of preventive behavior and specific SEA/SH provisions included in LMP. NFWP has established mechanisms for safe and ethical handling of SEA/SH allegations. GBV Service Provider mapping and referral pathways developed by NFWP available.

identified_risks.risk_description: Insecurity situation in Nigeria, especially in participating states (Taraba noted as conflict-affected), poses safety and security risks to workers and project beneficiaries during training activities.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Plan will rely on security information provided under NFWP when organizing training. NFWP developed Security Risk Management Plan to support activities. Security Officer deployed part-time to support grant activities and ensure adherence with NFWP's security management plan.

identified_risks.risk_description: Entrenched gender-discriminatory social norms have potential to put women who challenge these norms (by earning significant incomes or engaging in nontraditional employment) at risk of experiencing GBV, especially intimate partner violence (IPV), at least in short term.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Communications campaigns based on international good practices for behavior change and sensitization to address drivers of IPV and harassment. Improved livelihoods reducing conflict-inducing tensions such as poverty and resource scarcity. Safe spaces for women to discuss and support one another. Work with traditional structures (traditional/ward heads) as first responders to GBV cases. Training of facilitators to respond to GBV issues raised in dialogues. Women have access to NFWP GRM including phone hotline to report issues.

identified_risks.risk_description: NFWP PCUs had capacity gaps contributing to implementation delays in original six states; new States under Scale Up will require startup capacity building.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Component 1 specifically designed to provide targeted technical assistance through deployment of approximately 8 full-time technical experts for 12-18 months. Capacity assessments will identify specific gaps at each PCU. Regular technical review meetings with NFWP leadership, World Bank, and Ministry of Women Affairs. Feedback meetings with Technical Experts as accountability mechanism.

identified_risks.risk_description: Plan International lacks experience with new World Bank Procurement Framework and understanding of Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) preparation.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: World Bank will provide hands-on support and procurement capacity building during implementation support missions. Training on use of Systematic Tracking of Exchanges in Procurement (STEP). Task Team will develop specific measures to enhance contract management practices including list of procurement and contract execution 'red flags' and sanctionable practices. Monitoring of specific weaknesses and accountability for accurate and timely information on integrity risk management.

identified_risks.risk_description: Overall FM risk assessed as Low based on November 2021 assessment, but residual risks remain related to new staff and systems.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Plan will hire Finance Officer with professional experience and qualifications satisfactory to Bank. World Bank will train FM staff on Bank's Financial Management procedures and Disbursement Guidelines. Internal Audit Unit will conduct risk-based internal audits and prepare reports shared with IDA within 45 days after each calendar semester. Segregated Designated Account (DA) in USD and Naira Draw Down Account with reputable commercial bank. Bi-annual Interim Financial Reports submitted to IDA within 45 days after end of each calendar semester. Annual audits by independent qualified auditors acceptable to World Bank.

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Moderate (overall); Low for environmental risks, Moderate for social risks
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - NFWP has established GRM mechanisms. Project will operationalize NFWP's GRM which includes phone hotline for reporting issues. GBV Service Provider mapping and referral pathways developed by NFWP available. LMP includes GRM specifically for project workers to raise issues. World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available for project-affected communities and individuals; World Bank Inspection Panel also available for complaints regarding Bank non-compliance with policies and procedures.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project specifically targets women's social and economic empowerment. Women Affinity Groups (WAGs) are community-based women-only groups. Project addresses gender-discriminatory social norms limiting women's economic opportunities and access to productive assets. Gender dialogue groups with WAG members and families/intimate partners on joint financial planning, communication, negotiation skills, and joint decision-making. GBV awareness raising and prevention integrated throughout. Gender Manager (GBV) on project team. Gender considerations mainstreamed across all components. Project recognizes distinct gender-related vulnerabilities across Nigerian regions (child marriage, wife beating, restricted movement more pronounced in north; sexual violence, conflict-related GBV, SEA higher in north; FGM, IPV, trafficking, harmful widowhood practices more prevalent in south).
vulnerable_groups: Girls and young women (noted as having fewer educational opportunities, limited access to credit and productive resources, poorer labor market outcomes)
Women in rural areas (limited access to finance, prefer informal savings schemes)
Women in conflict-affected areas (Taraba, northeast - vulnerable to targeted violence by Boko Haram, abduction, forced marriages, stigmatization)
Women experiencing gender-based violence (31% of women aged 15-49 experienced physical or sexual violence; 14% experienced physical violence in 12 months prior to 2018 DHS)
Women in poverty (over 70% of women live below poverty line)
Out-of-school children (two-thirds of 10.5 million out-of-school children are girls)
Displaced persons and refugees (implied through conflict context and GBV risks)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project demonstrates conflict sensitivity through: (1) Recognition of conflict context - document notes heightened insecurity in Nigeria, conflict in northeast with Boko Haram activities targeting women and children for abduction, violence, forced marriages, and suicide bombing; (2) Inclusion of conflict-affected state (Taraba) in implementation; (3) Security risk management - NFWP Security Risk Management Plan referenced; security officer deployed; reliance on NFWP security information when organizing training; (4) GBV focus - project addresses conflict-related GBV and SEA; (5) Social norms programming - addresses norms that perpetuate violence and restrict women's participation; (6) Coordination with NFWP which has GBV Service Provider mapping and referral pathways; (7) Stakeholder Engagement Plan (SEP) prepared addressing security and COVID-19 constraints.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria context characterized by: (1) Stagnated development progress since 2015 due to oil price decline, increased insecurity, macroeconomic policy reversals, COVID-19 pandemic; (2) Subdued economic growth (1.1% average 2015-2021) coupled with rapid population growth (2.6% annually) widening GDP per capita gap; (3) Heightened insecurity as key threat to growth outlook; (4) Uncertain economic outlook dependent on external factors and government policy response; (5) Conflict in northeast (Boko Haram) with targeted violence against women and children; (6) Poor human capital outcomes - Nigeria 168th out of 174 countries on Human Capital Index; (7) Severe gender inequality - 139th out of 156 on Global Gender Gap Index; (8) High poverty - 80 million people living in poverty, over 70% of women below poverty line; (9) High maternal mortality (576 per 100,000); (10) Discriminatory social norms and institutions - among 10% of countries worldwide with highest gender discrimination per OECD Social Institutions and Gender Index; (11) Weak institutional capacity - NFWP PCUs had capacity gaps contributing to implementation delays; (12) Upcoming February 2023 general elections creating policy uncertainty.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security considerations explicitly addressed: (1) Heightened insecurity noted as threat to economic growth and development; (2) Conflict in northeast with Boko Haram activities; (3) Women and children targeted for abduction, violence, forced marriages, suicide bombing; (4) Women vulnerable to stigmatization and rejection from families and communities post-conflict; (5) NFWP Security Risk Management Plan developed and referenced; (6) Plan will rely on NFWP security information when organizing training; (7) Security Officer deployed part-time to support grant activities and ensure adherence with NFWP security management plan; (8) Safety and security of workers and beneficiaries during training emphasized as important consideration; (9) Taraba state (conflict-affected) included in implementation.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Document references conflict-related displacement and vulnerability: (1) Boko Haram conflict in northeast has contributed to steep rise in targeted violence against women and children; (2) Women increasingly used as instruments of war including suicide bombing and forced marriages; (3) Women vulnerable to stigmatization and rejection from families and communities; (4) Conflict-related GBV and SEA noted as higher in north; (5) While not explicitly quantified in this document, the context of Boko Haram insurgency in northeast Nigeria (where Taraba is located) is well-documented as causing significant displacement. Project addresses vulnerability of conflict-affected women through GBV prevention, social norms change, and economic empowerment activities.
